title: Trauma creep
description:
From a broken life to a broken nail, ‘trauma’ has been bleached by overuse. But it names something real – and must be reclaimed

- by Lily Dunn
hook: How 'trauma' became a catch-all diagnosis for modern anxiety—and what we lost when therapy language colonized everyday speech.

title: With ‘Girls Like Girls,’ Hayley Kiyoko Turns Her Teenage Pain Into Art
description: The once-closeted star has reinvented her song “Girls Like Girls” as a best-selling Y.A. novel and a new theatrical film. It wasn’t easy.
hook: She weaponized her closeted teenage years into a Y.A. novel and film. Now 'Girls Like Girls' means something entirely different.

title: Porous Encounters: A Queer East Correspondence
description: In collaboration with the Queer East Film Festival, this year’s Emerging Critics cohort offer their responses to the film programme.
hook: Young critics on queer East Asian cinema: what happens when you refuse the Western gaze and just watch what moves you.

title: Dancing on Moonbeams: Artist Luke Fowler on Electronic Music Pioneer Janet Beat
description:
Derek Jarman award-winner and Turner Prize nominee Luke Fowler speaks to Claire M. Holdsworth about his latest film, A Sensation Never Yet Known, a portrait of the electroacoustic composer and musician Janet Beat

Merging art and documentary, the films of Glasgow-based artist and musician Luke Fowler examine people who are dismis
hook: A Turner Prize nominee on Janet Beat, the electronic music pioneer nobody talks about—and why her erasure matters.

title: Americans Have Been Sold False Hope
description:
“Swan Song for the Republic,” excerpted from Freedom by Zinzi Clemmons We are born into good times. “Dream big,” they tell us. “The sky’s the limit.” We are lied to. One day at school, I’m shepherded into the band practice room, the TV set rolled out of the closet and switched on. On the screen […]

The post Americans Have Been Sold False Hope appeared first on
hook: Zinzi Clemmons on the specific lie America tells its children: dream big, then watch the system dismantle you anyway.

title: Operation Kill Everybody
description: The war on drugs is a politics of extermination.
hook: The war on drugs was never about drugs. It was always a politics of extermination targeting specific bodies and neighborhoods.

title: Everlane and the downfall of “millennial” brands
description: It was the sale heard around the millennial world: Eco-conscious Everlane was being acquired by fast-fashion kingpin Shein.  After a few tumultuous years, Everlane, an apparel brand loved largely by millennials looking for sustainable basics, reportedly went for the price of $100 million. When news broke last month, segments of the internet were up in […]
hook: When Everlane—the millennial symbol of conscious consumption—got bought by Shein, the entire aesthetic collapsed overnight.
